html:
Feathers are Items obtained by killing Birds. There are three feather types; Jet Feather, Crimson Feather, and Azure Feather. They can be obtained by killing birds by weapons or by "Murdering" them in the inventory after capturing them alive. Each bird has a 50% chance to drop its feather, excluding Pengulls, which have only a 20% chance of dropping theirs.

Contents

  • 1 Downloadable Content
  • 2 Jet Feather
  • 3 Crimson Feather
  • 4 Azure Feather
  • 5 Thunder Feather
  • 6 Usage
  • 7 Trivia
  • 8 Gallery

Downloadable Content

In all DLCs, Feathers can be used as fuel, providing about 15 seconds of fire in a Fire Pit.

In the Reign of Giants DLC, all 3 feather types can be found by destroying Tumbleweeds or as a "gift" from a Catcoon. Tumbleweeds have a mere 0.33% chance of dropping up to 3 Feathers. The DLC also introduces Buzzards, which have a 33% chance of dropping a Jet Feather when killed.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, all 3 feather types can be found by digging Sandy Piles with a 1% chance. The DLC also replaces the birds from the base game with different ones of the same color, red Parrots, black Toucans, Cormorants, and white Seagulls. The DLC also introduces Parrot Pirate, which drops a Crimson Feather when killed. Eyeshots can be used to capture birds alive.

Jet Feather

Exclusive to:  

Crimson Feather

Exclusive to:  

Azure Feather

Exclusive to:  

Thunder Feather

Exclusive to:

In the Hamlet DLC, the Thunder Feather is obtained by killing Thunderbirds. It is used to craft the Thunderhat. All three normal feather types also have a 4% chance of dropping from Smashing Pots.

Usage

Trivia

  • Before the Strange New Powers update, Crimson Feathers were called Redbird Feathers, Jet Feathers were called Crow Feathers, and Azure Feathers were called Snowbird Feathers. This has been changed thanks to the addition of Pengulls since they also drop Jet Feathers.
  • Saffron Feathers were introduced in the Warts and All update.

Gallery

  • Jet Feather Without Shadow
  • Crimson Feather Without Shadow
  • Azure Feather Without Shadow
  • Original HD Jet Feather icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Crimson Feather icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
plainText:
Don't Starve
Together
Feathers are Items obtained by killing Birds. There are three feather types; Jet Feather, Crimson Feather, and Azure Feather. They can be obtained by killing birds by weapons or by "Murdering" them in the inventory after capturing them alive. Each bird has a 50% chance to drop its feather, excluding Pengulls, which have only a 20% chance of dropping theirs.
Contents
1 Downloadable Content
2 Jet Feather
3 Crimson Feather
4 Azure Feather
5 Thunder Feather
6 Usage
7 Trivia
8 Gallery
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In all DLCs, Feathers can be used as fuel, providing about 15 seconds of fire in a Fire Pit.
In the Reign of Giants DLC, all 3 feather types can be found by destroying Tumbleweeds or as a "gift" from a Catcoon. Tumbleweeds have a mere 0.33% chance of dropping up to 3 Feathers. The DLC also introduces Buzzards, which have a 33% chance of dropping a Jet Feather when killed.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, all 3 feather types can be found by digging Sandy Piles with a 1% chance. The DLC also replaces the birds from the base game with different ones of the same color, red Parrots, black Toucans, Cormorants, and white Seagulls. The DLC also introduces Parrot Pirate, which drops a Crimson Feather when killed. Eyeshots can be used to capture birds alive.

“A crow feather.”
–Wilson, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Crow feather. It probably smells terrible on fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Is feather of bird black.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A crow feather, black like eternal darkness.”
–Wendy, when examining a Jet Feather.
“IT HAS BEEN SEPARATED FROM ITS BIRD”
–WX-78, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Feather of Corvus.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A gross feather.”
–Woodie, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Black is the color of sleep.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Interesting! Feathers appear to have properties wholly unique from each other.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A töken fröm the ravens!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Feather of black.”
–Webber, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A real softy.”
–Walani, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A bird's feather, in truffle black.”
–Warly, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Thet feather be mighty oily.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Jet Feather.
“'TIS BIRDY PART”
–Wilba, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Caw Tweeter clothes”
–Wormwood, when examining a Jet Feather.
“I'll keep it to remind me of how I'm not flying.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Not a whole lotta use for that.”
–Winona, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A memento of my jet feathered friend.”
–Wortox, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Black birdy feather.”
–Wurt, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Looks like a black bird feather.”
–Walter, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A black feather.”
–Wanda, when examining a Jet Feather.

–Wormwood, when examining a Thunder Feather.

–Wheeler, when examining a Thunder Feather.
In the Hamlet DLC, the Thunder Feather is obtained by killing Thunderbirds. It is used to craft the Thunderhat. All three normal feather types also have a 4% chance of dropping from Smashing Pots.

Before the Strange New Powers update, Crimson Feathers were called Redbird Feathers, Jet Feathers were called Crow Feathers, and Azure Feathers were called Snowbird Feathers. This has been changed thanks to the addition of Pengulls since they also drop Jet Feathers.
Saffron Feathers were introduced in the Warts and All update.

title: Feather
wikitext:
{{Navbutton/Separation}}
'''Feathers''' are [[Items]] obtained by killing [[Birds]]. There are three feather types; '''Jet Feather''', '''Crimson Feather''', and '''Azure Feather'''. They can be obtained by killing birds by weapons or by "Murdering" them in the inventory after [[Bird Trap|capturing]] them alive. Each bird has a 50% chance to drop its feather, excluding [[Pengull]]s, which have only a 20% chance of dropping theirs.
=={{pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==

In all [[DLC]]s, Feathers can be used as fuel, providing about 15 seconds of fire in a [[Fire Pit]].

In the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, all 3 feather types can be found by destroying [[Tumbleweed]]s or as a "gift" from a [[Catcoon]]. [[Tumbleweed]]s have a mere 0.33% chance of dropping up to 3 Feathers. The DLC also introduces [[Buzzards]], which have a 33% chance of dropping a Jet Feather when killed.

In the ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, all 3 feather types can be found by [[Shovel|digging]] [[Sandy Pile]]s with a 1% chance. The DLC also replaces the birds from the base game with different ones of the same color, red [[Parrot]]s, black [[Toucan]]s, [[Cormorant]]s, and white [[Seagull]]s. The DLC also introduces [[Parrot Pirate]], which drops a Crimson Feather when killed. [[Eyeshot]]s can be used to capture birds alive.

In the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC, the '''Thunder Feather''' is obtained by killing [[Thunderbird]]s. It is used to craft the [[Thunderhat]]. All three normal feather types also have a 4% chance of dropping from [[A Smashing Pot|Smashing Pots]].

=={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Before the [[New Features#July 2.2C 2013 - Strange New Powers|''Strange New Powers'']] update, Crimson Feathers were called Redbird Feathers, Jet Feathers were called Crow Feathers, and Azure Feathers were called Snowbird Feathers. This has been changed thanks to the addition of [[Pengull]]s since they also drop Jet Feathers.
* Saffron Feathers were introduced in the ''[[A New Reign#September 23rd, 2016 - Warts and All|Warts and All]]'' update.
